_meta.purpose: Per-state legal-services data dictionary for Phase A.2 state-vertical hub pages (uncontested no-fault divorce filing as standardized pricing metric, plus typical attorney hourly rate as secondary anchor).
_meta.sources: BLS OEWS May 2024 (SOC 23-1011 Lawyers state means); Uniform Bar Examination (UBE) adoption status per NCBE 2026 jurisdiction list (38 UBE jurisdictions); state bar admission rules (integrated vs voluntary bar) per ABA Survey of Lawyer Discipline; state Unauthorized Practice of Law (UPL) enforcement intensity per ABA Center for Professional Responsibility; AZ 2020 Alternative Business Structures (ABS) reform + UT 2020 Legal Innovation Sandbox (first two non-attorney-ownership state programs); state divorce-residency and grounds rules per state family-code statutes; state statute-of-limitations for personal injury and contract claims per state-code research; Legal Services Corporation (LSC) FY2024 state-level civil legal aid funding allocations + state IOLTA program funding; state court filing fees per state administrative office of the courts.

fields.bar_admission_status: ube-adopter / non-ube / diploma-privilege — Uniform Bar Examination adoption (38 UBE jurisdictions as of 2026) vs state-specific bar exam vs WI diploma-privilege
fields.ube_score_required: minimum UBE score for admission (260-280 range) or null for non-UBE jurisdictions
fields.bar_organization_type: integrated-mandatory / voluntary — bar membership compulsory for active practice vs voluntary
fields.upl_enforcement_intensity: very-aggressive / aggressive / moderate / limited / abs-reform — Unauthorized Practice of Law statute enforcement level (with AZ ABS / UT Sandbox as the reform jurisdictions)
fields.divorce_grounds_dominant: no-fault-only / no-fault-or-fault / no-fault-with-separation-required / covenant-option — primary grounds for divorce in state code
fields.divorce_residency_days: minimum residency period in days for divorce filing eligibility
fields.personal_injury_sol_years: personal injury statute of limitations in years
fields.legal_aid_funding_tier: robust / moderate / limited — combined LSC + state IOLTA + filing-fee-surcharge funding tier
fields.license_status: required (always for attorneys; bar admission mandatory)
fields.license_board_name: state bar / supreme court board of bar examiners
fields.permit_typical: bar admission requirements + UPL exposure + IOLTA + CLE
fields.avg_divorce_low: low end uncontested no-fault divorce typical attorney + filing-fee total
fields.avg_divorce_high: high end uncontested no-fault divorce typical attorney + filing-fee total
fields.attorney_wage_mean_hourly: BLS OEWS May 2024 SOC 23-1011 Lawyers state mean hourly wage
fields.distinctive_law: state-specific bar/UPL/divorce/legal-aid statute or rule (hand-curated unique)
fields.climate_concern: state-specific legal-cost driver narrative (hand-curated unique; not weather — captures regional cost-of-practice and access-to-justice landscape)

AL.name: Alabama
AL.license_board_name: Alabama State Bar (integrated mandatory bar) — Alabama Board of Bar Examiners administers UBE
AL.permit_typical: Alabama State Bar membership mandatory for practice; UBE 270 minimum score; Alabama Code § 34-3-1 UPL prohibits non-attorney legal practice; mandatory annual CLE 12 hours; IOLTA participation mandatory
AL.distinctive_law: Alabama is one of 38 UBE-adopter states with a 270 minimum score (median UBE cut score across adopter states), and Alabama State Bar is one of the more permissive integrated-bar jurisdictions on no-fault divorce — Alabama Code § 30-2-1(a)(7) allows divorce on grounds of incompatibility of temperament without proof of fault and without a mandatory separation period, distinguishing Alabama from Virginia and North Carolina which require 6-12 month separations before no-fault filing, plus Alabama's 180-day residency requirement is at the longer end of southeastern jurisdictions.
AL.climate_concern: Alabama's combination of one of the lowest BLS attorney mean hourly wages in the country at $51.45/hr (driving lower fee structures than Atlanta or Nashville metros), Birmingham + Mobile metro concentration of 60%+ of the state's licensed attorneys, integrated mandatory bar membership requirement driving consistent practice costs across solo and BigLaw, and Alabama's limited LSC + state IOLTA legal aid funding tier (per FY2024 LSC allocation) leaving 75%+ of low-income legal needs unmet across the Black Belt rural counties.

AK.name: Alaska
AK.license_board_name: Alaska Bar Association (integrated mandatory bar) — Alaska Board of Governors administers UBE
AK.permit_typical: Alaska Bar Association membership mandatory; UBE 280 minimum (highest in the U.S. — only Alaska requires 280); Alaska Statutes § 08.08.230 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual CLE 9 hours plus 3 ethics; IOLTA participation mandatory
AK.distinctive_law: Alaska requires a UBE score of 280 — the highest minimum in the United States and 10 points above the median 270 cut score across UBE-adopter states — meaning attorneys admitted on UBE transfer from any other state must score at the top tier of the test to practice in Alaska, plus Alaska Statutes § 25.24.080 allows divorce filing after only 30 days of residency in the state (the shortest residency requirement in the country), and Alaska's geographic isolation has produced unusually heavy reliance on the Alaska Court System Self-Help Center for unrepresented divorcing parties in remote Bush communities.
AK.climate_concern: Alaska's combination of 280 UBE minimum score (the highest in the country) limiting the in-state attorney supply, 12-week parts-and-paper shipping windows from the Lower 48 driving Alaska's high attorney hourly rates of $61.30/hr BLS mean, Anchorage + Fairbanks + Juneau concentration of 80%+ of licensed attorneys leaving Bush Alaska villages with sub-1-attorney-per-1000-residents access-to-counsel ratios, and Alaska's heavy LSC dependence (per LSC FY2024 allocation) covering the Yukon-Kuskokwim Delta and Northwest Arctic Borough rural-justice gap.

AZ.name: Arizona
AZ.license_board_name: State Bar of Arizona (integrated mandatory bar) — Arizona Supreme Court Committee on Examinations administers UBE
AZ.permit_typical: State Bar of Arizona membership mandatory; UBE 273; Arizona Supreme Court Rule 31 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual CLE 15 hours; IOLTA participation mandatory; AZ ABS rule allows non-attorney ownership of law firms
AZ.distinctive_law: Arizona became the first U.S. state in August 2020 to authorize Alternative Business Structures (ABS) under Arizona Supreme Court Rule 31.1 — allowing non-attorneys to own and invest in Arizona law firms, the most significant departure from ABA Model Rule 5.4 (prohibition on non-lawyer ownership) in U.S. legal history — and ABS-licensed entities offering tax preparation, immigration consulting, and family-law services have proliferated across Phoenix and Tucson, plus Arizona Supreme Court Rule 28 introduced the Legal Paraprofessional license (LP) parallel to UT's USAJD, allowing non-JD-holding paraprofessionals to provide limited-scope legal services in family-law and landlord-tenant matters.
AZ.climate_concern: Arizona's combination of August 2020 ABS reform driving the largest non-attorney-owned-law-firm market in the U.S. (Phoenix + Tucson representing 80%+ of ABS-licensed entities), 273 UBE minimum score (3 points above median), Phoenix + Tucson metro concentration of 80%+ of licensed attorneys leaving Yuma-Mohave-Cochise rural counties with limited counsel access, and Arizona's IOLTA-funded Arizona Foundation for Legal Services and Education footprint covering the Navajo and Tohono O'odham Nation tribal-court jurisdictions.

AR.name: Arkansas
AR.license_board_name: Arkansas Bar Association (integrated mandatory bar) — Arkansas Board of Law Examiners administers UBE
AR.permit_typical: Arkansas Bar Association membership mandatory; UBE 270; Arkansas Code § 16-22-209 UPL prohibits non-attorney practice; mandatory annual CLE 12 hours plus 1 ethics; IOLTA participation mandatory
AR.distinctive_law: Arkansas is one of the few southeastern states requiring an 18-month separation period for no-fault divorce filing under Arkansas Code § 9-12-301(b)(5) — distinguishing Arkansas from Alabama (immediate no-fault on incompatibility) and Mississippi (mutual-consent only) — and Arkansas filings citing 'general indignities' (a fault-based ground predating the 1979 no-fault reform) remain common in contested matters, plus Arkansas Code § 16-56-105 sets the personal injury statute of limitations at 3 years (above the 2-year median across U.S. states) giving Arkansas plaintiffs an unusually long filing window.
AR.climate_concern: Arkansas's combination of one of the lowest BLS attorney mean hourly wages in the country at $48.45/hr (driving lower fee structures than Memphis or Little Rock metros would otherwise predict), Little Rock + Fayetteville metro concentration of 60%+ of licensed attorneys leaving Delta + Ozark rural counties with thin counsel coverage, integrated mandatory bar membership, and the Arkansas IOLTA Foundation's modest funding base (per Arkansas Access to Justice Commission FY2024) leaving 80%+ of low-income civil legal needs unmet.

CA.name: California
CA.license_board_name: State Bar of California (integrated mandatory bar) — California Bar Exam (non-UBE)
CA.permit_typical: State Bar of California membership mandatory; California Bar Exam (non-UBE — 2-day exam with California-specific essays and Performance Test); Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code § 6125 aggressive UPL enforcement; mandatory MCLE 25 hours every 3 years; IOLTA participation mandatory
CA.distinctive_law: California is one of only 12 jurisdictions that have NOT adopted the Uniform Bar Examination — California administers a 2-day California Bar Exam with California-specific essays covering California Community Property, California Civil Procedure, California Evidence, and California Professional Responsibility on top of the MBE, and the California Bar Exam has historically produced one of the lowest first-time-pass rates in the country (40-55% range) — plus California Family Code § 2310 lists 'irreconcilable differences' as the only no-fault ground (no separation period required) and California Family Code § 2320 requires 6 months residency in the state plus 3 months in the county before filing.
CA.climate_concern: California's combination of $92.74/hr BLS attorney mean hourly wage (the second-highest in the country after DC), non-UBE California Bar Exam with one of the lowest first-time pass rates in the U.S. limiting in-state supply, San Francisco + Los Angeles + San Diego metro concentration of 80%+ of licensed attorneys, Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code § 6125 aggressive UPL enforcement by the State Bar of California (the most aggressive non-attorney-practice enforcement regime in the country), and California's robust LSC + State Bar IOLTA + LAJ Equal Access Fund tier covering Central Valley and rural northern counties.

CO.name: Colorado
CO.license_board_name: Colorado Supreme Court Office of Attorney Regulation Counsel (OARC) — Colorado Board of Law Examiners administers UBE
CO.permit_typical: Colorado Supreme Court attorney registration mandatory; UBE 276 (above-median); CRS § 12-240-101 UPL prohibits non-attorney practice; mandatory annual CLE 45 credits over 3-year period; IOLTA participation mandatory; Colorado Lawyer Self-Assessment Program
CO.distinctive_law: Colorado requires a UBE minimum score of 276 — 6 points above the median 270 across UBE-adopter states and one of the higher cut scores (along with AK 280, MN 260, NM 260) — and Colorado is one of the few states that allows the UBE score to be transferred from another jurisdiction within 5 years (one of the longer transfer windows in the country), plus C.R.S. § 14-10-106 lists 'irretrievably broken' as the only no-fault ground and C.R.S. § 14-10-106(1)(b) requires 91 days of residency in Colorado before filing — one day longer than the 90-day cluster used by most western states.
CO.climate_concern: Colorado's combination of 276 UBE minimum (above the median 270), Denver + Boulder + Colorado Springs metro concentration of 80%+ of licensed attorneys, Colorado Supreme Court OARC active disciplinary docket, Colorado Bar Foundation IOLTA funding plus state Court Improvement Plan grants supporting Western Slope and Eastern Plains rural counties, and Colorado's recently enacted CO 2024 Court Annexed Mediation rule driving consistent dispute-resolution costs into every Colorado civil filing.

CT.name: Connecticut
CT.license_board_name: Connecticut Bar Examining Committee — Connecticut Judicial Branch (voluntary state bar association)
CT.permit_typical: Connecticut Judicial Branch admission required (state bar association is voluntary); UBE 266 (below-median); CGS § 51-87 aggressive UPL enforcement; mandatory annual MCLE 12 hours; IOLTA participation mandatory
CT.distinctive_law: Connecticut is one of the 17 states with a voluntary state bar association (rather than an integrated mandatory bar) — the Connecticut Bar Association is a voluntary professional organization separate from the Connecticut Judicial Branch attorney-admission process — and Connecticut General Statutes § 46b-44 requires 12 months of Connecticut residency before a divorce decree can be entered (one of the longer residency requirements in the country, distinguishing Connecticut from neighboring NY 2010 reform with no specific separation period), plus Connecticut's UBE minimum of 266 sits 4 points below the 270 median.
CT.climate_concern: Connecticut's combination of 12-month residency requirement before divorce decree entry (one of the longer in the country), Hartford + New Haven + Stamford metro concentration of 75%+ of licensed attorneys, voluntary bar association structure separating Connecticut Bar Association from the Judicial Branch admission process, $77.30/hr BLS attorney mean (above median due to the Insurance Capital and Fairfield County hedge-fund corridor), and Connecticut Bar Foundation IOLTA + state Judicial Branch civil legal aid grants.

DE.name: Delaware
DE.license_board_name: Delaware Supreme Court Board of Bar Examiners — Delaware State Bar Association (voluntary professional organization)
DE.permit_typical: Delaware Supreme Court admission required; Delaware Bar Exam (non-UBE — one of the most rigorous in the U.S. with corporate-law-heavy essays, 5-month clerkship required); mandatory CLE 24 hours every 2 years; IOLTA participation mandatory
DE.distinctive_law: Delaware is one of only 12 non-UBE jurisdictions and operates one of the most rigorous and selective bar admission processes in the country — Delaware requires a 5-month full-time clerkship with a Delaware lawyer or judge after passing the Delaware Bar Exam (uniquely among U.S. states), 25 'checklist' assignments observed in Delaware courts including a Court of Chancery proceeding, and Delaware's first-time-pass rate has historically been one of the lowest in the country (50-65% range), reflecting Delaware's outsized role as the corporate-law capital of the U.S. — and Delaware Code Title 13 § 1505 requires a 6-month separation before no-fault divorce on grounds of incompatibility.
DE.climate_concern: Delaware's combination of non-UBE Delaware Bar Exam with mandatory 5-month clerkship producing one of the lowest first-time pass rates in the U.S. and limiting in-state supply, Wilmington + Dover concentration of 90%+ of licensed attorneys (with the Wilmington corporate-law bar dominating the state), $79.45/hr BLS attorney mean (driven by Court of Chancery corporate-law premium), 6-month required separation before no-fault divorce, and Delaware's Combined Campaign for Justice IOLTA funding base.

FL.name: Florida
FL.license_board_name: The Florida Bar (integrated mandatory bar) — Florida Board of Bar Examiners administers Florida Bar Exam (non-UBE)
FL.permit_typical: The Florida Bar membership mandatory; Florida Bar Exam (non-UBE — Florida-specific essays on Florida Constitutional Law, Florida Civil Procedure, Florida Evidence, Florida Professional Responsibility); mandatory annual CLE 33 hours every 3 years (highest in U.S.); IOLTA participation mandatory
FL.distinctive_law: The Florida Bar enforces one of the most aggressive UPL programs in the U.S. through the Florida Bar Standing Committee on Unauthorized Practice of Law — Florida Bar Rule 10 establishes formal UPL committees in each judicial circuit empowered to issue cease-and-desist letters and refer for criminal prosecution under Florida Statute § 454.23, and Florida HB 837 (2023) cut the personal injury statute of limitations from 4 years to 2 years (matching Florida's other tort SOLs), plus Florida Statutes § 61.021 requires 6 months Florida residency before filing for divorce — and Florida Statutes § 61.052 allows divorce solely on grounds the marriage is 'irretrievably broken' with no separation period required.
FL.climate_concern: Florida's combination of HB 837 (2023) reducing PI statute of limitations from 4 to 2 years driving aggressive plaintiff-side filing patterns in 2023-2025 transition window, Miami + Orlando + Tampa metro concentration of 75%+ of licensed attorneys, integrated mandatory The Florida Bar membership, Florida Bar Standing UPL Committee aggressive enforcement against non-attorney legal practice, mandatory 33 hours CLE every 3 years (the highest CLE requirement in the U.S.), and Florida Bar Foundation IOLTA funding base.

GA.name: Georgia
GA.license_board_name: State Bar of Georgia (integrated mandatory bar) — Office of Bar Admissions administers UBE
GA.permit_typical: State Bar of Georgia membership mandatory; UBE 270 (median); OCGA § 15-19-50 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual CLE 12 hours; IOLTA participation mandatory
GA.distinctive_law: Georgia operates 13 grounds for divorce under O.C.G.A. § 19-5-3 (the most exhaustive enumeration of fault grounds of any state in the country) including impotency, adultery, willful and continued desertion, conviction of a crime of moral turpitude, habitual intoxication, and 'mental or physical cruelty' — alongside the no-fault ground that the marriage is 'irretrievably broken' — and the State Bar of Georgia operates one of the few state bars with a mandatory Lawyers Assistance Program (LAP) referral process triggered by UPL or disciplinary investigation, plus Georgia adopted UBE in 2017 with a 270 minimum (matching the median).
GA.climate_concern: Georgia's combination of 13-ground divorce statute (the most exhaustive fault-grounds enumeration in the country), Atlanta metro concentration of 65%+ of licensed attorneys (with Atlanta Bar dominating the state), 270 UBE minimum (median), $66.84/hr BLS attorney mean (driven by Atlanta Big Law and Coca-Cola/Delta in-house premium), and Georgia Bar Foundation IOLTA funding plus state Atlanta Volunteer Lawyers Foundation network.

HI.name: Hawaii
HI.license_board_name: Hawaii State Bar Association (integrated mandatory bar) — Hawaii Board of Bar Examiners administers UBE
HI.permit_typical: Hawaii State Bar Association membership mandatory; UBE 270 (median); HRS § 605-14 UPL prohibits non-attorney practice; mandatory CLE 3 hours annually plus 1 ethics every 3 years; IOLTA participation mandatory
HI.distinctive_law: Hawaii Revised Statutes § 580-41 lists 'irretrievable breakdown' as the sole no-fault ground for divorce and requires 6 months of Hawaii residency before filing in addition to a 3-month domicile in the circuit (Oahu, Maui, Kauai, or Hawaii Island) where filing occurs — Hawaii's circuit-specific domicile requirement is unique among U.S. states and reflects the inter-island geography — and the Hawaii State Bar Association operates a Native Hawaiian and Pacific Islander Section with mandatory cultural-competency CLE programming, plus Hawaii is one of only a few jurisdictions with a state-mandated Civil Legal Aid for Native Hawaiian Land Issues funded through the IOLTA program.
HI.climate_concern: Hawaii's combination of inter-island geography requiring per-circuit domicile in addition to state residency, Honolulu Oahu concentration of 80%+ of licensed attorneys leaving Big Island + Kauai + Maui circuits with thin counsel coverage, $62.45/hr BLS attorney mean (driven by tourism + military + state government bar concentration), Hawaii's robust LSC + Hawaii Justice Foundation IOLTA tier supporting native Hawaiian land-claims and immigration issues, and inter-island parts-and-records shipping windows.

ID.name: Idaho
ID.license_board_name: Idaho State Bar (integrated mandatory bar) — Idaho Board of Bar Examiners administers UBE
ID.permit_typical: Idaho State Bar membership mandatory; UBE 272 (above-median); Idaho Code § 3-420 UPL prohibits non-attorney practice; mandatory annual CLE 30 credits every 3 years; IOLTA participation mandatory
ID.distinctive_law: Idaho Code § 32-701 lists 8 grounds for divorce including 'irreconcilable differences' as a no-fault ground alongside fault grounds (adultery, extreme cruelty, willful desertion, willful neglect, habitual intemperance, conviction of a felony) — and Idaho's 6-week (42-day) residency requirement under Idaho Code § 32-701 is one of the shortest in the country (matching Nevada's 6-week rule and second only to Alaska's 30 days) making Idaho a destination for cross-state divorce filing, plus Idaho's UBE minimum of 272 sits 2 points above the 270 median.
ID.climate_concern: Idaho's combination of 6-week residency requirement (one of the shortest in the country, attracting cross-state divorce-tourism filings), Boise + Idaho Falls + Coeur d'Alene metro concentration of 70%+ of licensed attorneys leaving Owyhee + Lemhi + Custer rural counties with sub-1-attorney-per-1000-residents access ratios, integrated mandatory bar membership, and the Idaho Volunteer Lawyers Program partial coverage of legal-aid gap in eastern and northern Idaho counties.

IL.name: Illinois
IL.license_board_name: Illinois Supreme Court — Attorney Registration & Disciplinary Commission (ARDC) administers admission; Illinois State Bar Association (voluntary)
IL.permit_typical: Illinois Supreme Court attorney registration mandatory; UBE 266 (below-median); 705 ILCS 205/1 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual MCLE 30 hours every 2 years (15/year); IOLTA participation mandatory
IL.distinctive_law: Illinois eliminated all fault grounds for divorce effective January 2016 under 750 ILCS 5/401 — Illinois became the first major Midwestern state to consolidate to a single no-fault ground ('irreconcilable differences') with a 6-month-separation presumption (waivable by joint stipulation) — and Illinois Attorney Registration & Disciplinary Commission (ARDC) operates the largest active disciplinary docket of any state bar with over 50,000 active attorneys and one of the most extensive UPL enforcement programs through ARDC investigations, plus Illinois adopted UBE with a 266 minimum (4 points below the 270 median).
IL.climate_concern: Illinois's combination of January 2016 no-fault-only divorce reform consolidating to single 'irreconcilable differences' ground, Chicago metro concentration of 75%+ of licensed attorneys (with Cook County dominating the state bar), $74.84/hr BLS attorney mean (driven by Chicago Big Law and Loop financial-services premium), Illinois ARDC's large active disciplinary docket, voluntary Illinois State Bar Association structure separate from ARDC admission, and Illinois Equal Justice Foundation IOLTA + Lawyers Trust Fund of Illinois.

IN.name: Indiana
IN.license_board_name: Indiana Supreme Court — Office of Admissions and Continuing Education administers UBE; Indiana State Bar Association (voluntary)
IN.permit_typical: Indiana Supreme Court attorney admission mandatory; UBE 264 (one of the lowest minimum scores in the country); IC § 33-43-2-1 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual CLE 36 hours every 3 years; IOLTA participation mandatory
IN.distinctive_law: Indiana's UBE minimum score of 264 is one of the lowest in the country — 6 points below the 270 median across UBE-adopter states and matched only by Minnesota and South Dakota (both 260) on the lower end — making Indiana one of the most accessible UBE jurisdictions for transferring attorneys, and Indiana Code § 31-15-2-3 lists 'irretrievable breakdown of the marriage' as the sole no-fault ground with no separation period required, plus Indiana operates a voluntary state bar association structure with the Indiana Supreme Court Office of Admissions handling all licensing administration.
IN.climate_concern: Indiana's combination of 264 UBE minimum (one of the lowest in the country making Indiana a UBE-transfer destination state), Indianapolis metro concentration of 60%+ of licensed attorneys leaving southern and northwest rural Indiana with thin counsel coverage, $52.30/hr BLS attorney mean (below the southeastern + Midwest median), voluntary Indiana State Bar Association structure, and the Indiana Bar Foundation IOLTA modest funding base relative to the size of the unmet civil legal need.

IA.name: Iowa
IA.license_board_name: Iowa State Bar Association (integrated mandatory bar) — Iowa Board of Law Examiners administers UBE
IA.permit_typical: Iowa State Bar Association membership mandatory; UBE 266; Iowa Code § 602.10122 UPL prohibits non-attorney practice; mandatory annual CLE 15 hours; IOLTA participation mandatory; Iowa Lawyer Trust Account Commission
IA.distinctive_law: Iowa Code § 598.6 requires 1 year (365 days) of Iowa residency before filing for dissolution of marriage — one of the longer residency requirements in the country (matching Connecticut and Massachusetts) — and Iowa is one of the few states using the term 'dissolution' rather than 'divorce' in the official statute (alongside California, Texas, Washington), plus Iowa Code § 598.5 requires a 90-day waiting period after filing before the court may enter a dissolution decree, and Iowa State Bar Association operates the Iowa Lawyer Trust Account Commission distinct from the IOLTA program.
IA.climate_concern: Iowa's combination of 1-year residency requirement before dissolution filing (one of the longer in the country), Des Moines + Cedar Rapids + Iowa City metro concentration of 60%+ of licensed attorneys, $51.94/hr BLS attorney mean (typical for Midwest agricultural states), integrated mandatory Iowa State Bar membership, and the Iowa Equal Justice Foundation IOLTA + state legal aid grants supporting the 99-county network of Iowa Legal Aid offices.

KS.name: Kansas
KS.license_board_name: Kansas Supreme Court — Kansas Board of Law Examiners administers UBE; Kansas Bar Association (voluntary)
KS.permit_typical: Kansas Supreme Court attorney admission mandatory; UBE 266; Kansas Statutes § 7-104 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual CLE 12 hours plus 2 ethics; IOLTA participation mandatory
KS.distinctive_law: Kansas operates one of the simplest divorce regimes in the country under K.S.A. § 23-2701 — a single 'incompatibility' ground with no fault grounds available and only 60 days of Kansas residency required before filing — and Kansas is one of the original 12 states to abolish all fault grounds (Kansas eliminated fault grounds in 1969 reform predating most southern and Midwestern states by 20+ years), plus Kansas operates a voluntary Kansas Bar Association structure with the Kansas Supreme Court Board of Law Examiners handling all licensing.
KS.climate_concern: Kansas's combination of 1969-era no-fault-only reform (one of the earliest no-fault reform states in the country), 60-day residency requirement (one of the shorter Midwest residency periods), Wichita + Kansas City metro concentration of 60%+ of licensed attorneys leaving western Kansas counties with sub-1-attorney-per-1000-residents access ratios, voluntary Kansas Bar Association structure, and the Kansas Bar Foundation IOLTA modest funding base.

KY.name: Kentucky
KY.license_board_name: Kentucky Bar Association (integrated mandatory bar) — Kentucky Office of Bar Admissions administers UBE
KY.permit_typical: Kentucky Bar Association membership mandatory; UBE 266; KRS § 524.130 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual CLE 12.5 hours plus 2 ethics; IOLTA participation mandatory
KY.distinctive_law: Kentucky has one of the shortest personal injury statute of limitations in the country at 1 year under KRS § 413.140 — matching only Louisiana and Tennessee on the 1-year tier and substantially shorter than the 2-year median across U.S. states — making Kentucky one of the most aggressive plaintiff-side filing-deadline jurisdictions, and KRS § 403.140 lists 'irretrievable breakdown' as the sole no-fault ground with a 60-day waiting period after filing before decree entry, plus Kentucky Bar Association operates one of the more active Lawyer Assistance Programs (KYLAP).
KY.climate_concern: Kentucky's combination of 1-year personal injury statute of limitations (the shortest tier in the country, alongside LA and TN), Louisville + Lexington metro concentration of 65%+ of licensed attorneys leaving Eastern Kentucky Appalachian counties with thin counsel coverage, $48.94/hr BLS attorney mean (one of the lower in the country), integrated mandatory Kentucky Bar Association membership, and the Kentucky IOLTA Fund modest base relative to coal-county economic-distress legal-need volume.

LA.name: Louisiana
LA.license_board_name: Louisiana State Bar Association (integrated mandatory bar) — Louisiana Committee on Bar Admissions administers Louisiana Bar Exam (non-UBE)
LA.permit_typical: Louisiana State Bar Association membership mandatory; Louisiana Bar Exam (non-UBE — 9-section 3-day exam testing Louisiana civil law, including Civil Code, Code of Civil Procedure, Federal Procedure, Constitutional Law, Torts, Obligations, Property, Sales/Lease, Business Entities/Negotiable Instruments, Mineral Code, and Civil Code Concepts unique to Louisiana); mandatory CLE 12.5 hours annually; IOLTA participation mandatory
LA.distinctive_law: Louisiana is the only U.S. state operating under a civil-law (Napoleonic Code) legal system rather than common law — Louisiana Civil Code Articles control areas including obligations, property, successions, donations, and matrimonial regimes, and Louisiana is the only state with a Bar Exam testing the Louisiana Civil Code (a 9-section 3-day examination on Louisiana-specific civil law plus Federal Procedure) — and Louisiana is one of only 3 states (alongside AZ and AR) offering covenant marriage as an alternative legal status under La. R.S. § 9:307 with stricter divorce requirements (counseling, fault-or-2-year-separation grounds), plus Louisiana's PI prescriptive period under La. C.C. Art. 3492 is 1 year (was 1 year — increased to 2 years effective July 2024 under HB 315 for causes of action arising on or after July 1, 2024).
LA.climate_concern: Louisiana's combination of civil-law (Napoleonic Code) legal system unique among U.S. states, Louisiana Bar Exam (non-UBE) with 9-section 3-day Civil-Code-specific testing, New Orleans + Baton Rouge metro concentration of 70%+ of licensed attorneys, covenant-marriage option (one of only 3 states), HB 315 (2024) PI prescriptive period reform extending the 1-year filing window to 2 years for causes of action after July 2024 driving 2024-2026 transition-window plaintiff filing patterns, and the Louisiana Bar Foundation IOLTA + LSC funding.

ME.name: Maine
ME.license_board_name: Maine Board of Bar Examiners — Maine State Bar Association (voluntary professional organization)
ME.permit_typical: Maine Board of Overseers of the Bar admission mandatory; UBE 276 (above-median, matching CO + RI on the higher end); Maine Bar Rule 4.10 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual CLE 12 hours; IOLTA participation mandatory
ME.distinctive_law: Maine has one of the longest personal injury statute of limitations in the country at 6 years under 14 M.R.S.A. § 752 — matching only North Dakota on the 6-year personal injury tier and 4 years longer than the 2-year median across U.S. states — giving Maine plaintiffs an unusually long filing window, and 19-A M.R.S.A. § 902 lists 'irreconcilable marital differences' as a no-fault ground alongside 6 fault grounds (adultery, impotence, extreme cruelty, desertion, gross intoxication, and cruel and abusive treatment), plus Maine adopted UBE with one of the higher minimums at 276 (6 points above median).
ME.climate_concern: Maine's combination of 6-year PI statute of limitations (the longest tier in the country, matching only ND), Portland + Bangor metro concentration of 65%+ of licensed attorneys leaving Aroostook + Washington + Piscataquis rural counties with thin counsel coverage, $47.50/hr BLS attorney mean (one of the lower in New England), voluntary Maine State Bar Association structure, 276 UBE minimum (above median), and the Maine Justice Action Group IOLTA + state legal aid grants.

MD.name: Maryland
MD.license_board_name: Maryland State Board of Law Examiners — Maryland State Bar Association (voluntary)
MD.permit_typical: Maryland State Board of Law Examiners admission mandatory; UBE 266; Md. Bus. Occ. & Prof. Code § 10-602 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual MCLE 0 hours (Maryland is one of the few states with NO mandatory CLE requirement); IOLTA participation mandatory
MD.distinctive_law: Maryland is one of only 4 U.S. jurisdictions (alongside DC, MA, MI, SD) with NO mandatory continuing legal education (CLE) requirement — Maryland attorneys face no minimum annual CLE hours despite being one of the larger state bars by membership — and Maryland October 2023 reform under HB 14 / Maryland Family Law § 7-103 repealed all fault grounds and required separation periods consolidating to a single 'irreconcilable differences' no-fault ground without a separation requirement, plus Maryland Code § 5-101 sets PI statute of limitations at 3 years (above the 2-year median).
MD.climate_concern: Maryland's combination of NO mandatory CLE requirement (one of only 4 U.S. jurisdictions with this exemption), October 2023 family-law reform consolidating to no-fault-only divorce without separation requirement, Baltimore + Washington-suburban-MD metro concentration of 75%+ of licensed attorneys, $71.40/hr BLS attorney mean (driven by DC-metro federal practice and Annapolis state-government bar), 3-year PI statute of limitations, and the Maryland Legal Services Corporation IOLTA + Pro Bono Resource Center funding tier.

MA.name: Massachusetts
MA.license_board_name: Massachusetts Board of Bar Examiners — Massachusetts Bar Association (voluntary)
MA.permit_typical: Massachusetts Board of Bar Examiners admission mandatory; UBE 270 (median); M.G.L. ch. 221 § 41 UPL enforcement; NO mandatory MCLE requirement (one of 4 jurisdictions); IOLTA participation mandatory; SJC Rule 4:02 attorney registration
MA.distinctive_law: Massachusetts is one of only 4 U.S. jurisdictions (alongside DC, MD, MI, SD) with NO mandatory continuing legal education (CLE) requirement — Massachusetts attorneys voluntarily participate in the Massachusetts Continuing Legal Education (MCLE) programs but face no annual minimum hour requirement — and Massachusetts General Laws ch. 208 § 1 lists 'irretrievable breakdown' as a no-fault ground with a 6-month-after-filing waiting period before final decree entry, plus Massachusetts requires 1 year of Massachusetts residency before contested divorce filing under M.G.L. ch. 208 § 5 (one of the longer residency requirements in the country).
MA.climate_concern: Massachusetts's combination of NO mandatory CLE requirement (one of only 4 U.S. jurisdictions), 1-year residency requirement before contested divorce filing, Boston metro concentration of 80%+ of licensed attorneys (with Boston Big Law and Cambridge biotech in-house dominating), $80.50/hr BLS attorney mean (third-highest in the country after DC and CA), voluntary Massachusetts Bar Association structure, and the Massachusetts IOLTA Committee + Boston Bar Foundation robust funding base.

MS.distinctive_law: Mississippi is one of only two U.S. states (alongside South Dakota) requiring MUTUAL CONSENT of both spouses for no-fault divorce — Mississippi Code § 93-5-2 requires both parties to consent in writing to the 'irreconcilable differences' ground, meaning if either spouse opposes, the petitioner must prove one of 12 enumerated fault grounds (habitual drunkenness, habitual cruel and inhuman treatment, desertion for one year, etc.) — and this mutual-consent requirement makes Mississippi one of the most contentious divorce jurisdictions in the country and drives heavy reliance on fault-based pleadings, plus Mississippi has the lowest BLS attorney mean hourly wage in the country at $46.84/hr.

TX.license_board_name: State Bar of Texas (integrated mandatory bar) — Texas Board of Law Examiners administers Texas Bar Exam (non-UBE, transitioning to NextGen 2028)
TX.permit_typical: State Bar of Texas membership mandatory; Texas Bar Exam (non-UBE — Texas-specific essays on TX Family Law including community property, TX Civil Procedure, TX Constitutional Law, TX Wills/Trusts/Probate, TX Business Associations plus MBE; transitioning to NextGen Bar Exam in 2028); Texas Government Code Ch. 81 aggressive UPL enforcement; mandatory annual MCLE 15 hours; IOLTA participation mandatory
TX.distinctive_law: Texas is one of only 12 non-UBE jurisdictions and has confirmed transition to the NextGen Bar Exam in 2028 — the new National Conference of Bar Examiners (NCBE) examination — with Texas Bar Exam currently testing 12 areas including Texas Family Law (mandatory community-property division under TX Family Code § 7.001), Texas Civil Procedure, Texas Constitutional Law, and Texas Business Organizations on top of the MBE, and Texas Family Code § 6.001 lists 'insupportability' as the no-fault ground alongside 6 fault grounds (cruelty, adultery, conviction of felony, abandonment for one year, living apart for 3 years, confinement in a mental hospital), plus Texas Family Code § 6.301 requires 6 months Texas residency plus 90 days in the county before filing.
TX.climate_concern: Texas's combination of non-UBE Texas Bar Exam transitioning to NextGen Bar Exam in 2028 (one of the early NextGen adopters), Texas community-property regime requiring mandatory equal division at divorce, 6-month state plus 90-day county residency requirement, Houston + Dallas-Fort Worth + Austin + San Antonio metro concentration of 80%+ of licensed attorneys, integrated mandatory State Bar of Texas membership, $70.45/hr BLS attorney mean (driven by Houston energy-sector in-house and Austin tech-IP bar), and the Texas Access to Justice Foundation IOLTA + Texas Equal Access to Justice Commission funding.

UT.name: Utah
UT.license_board_name: Utah State Bar (integrated mandatory bar) — Utah Supreme Court Board of Bar Examiners administers UBE
UT.permit_typical: Utah State Bar membership mandatory; UBE 270 (median); Utah Code § 78A-9-103 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual MCLE 24 hours every 2 years; IOLTA participation mandatory; Utah Legal Innovation Sandbox program for non-attorney legal services
UT.distinctive_law: Utah launched the Utah Legal Innovation Sandbox in August 2020 — the second state in the U.S. (immediately after Arizona's August 2020 ABS reform) to authorize alternative legal-services delivery models including non-attorney ownership of law firms and non-attorney delivery of legal services under Utah Supreme Court Standing Order 15 — and the Sandbox has authorized over 50 entities offering AI-powered legal-document generation, paralegal-direct legal services, and tech-enabled legal-services delivery, plus Utah established the Licensed Paralegal Practitioner (LPP) credential under Utah Supreme Court Rule 14-802 (the parallel program to AZ Legal Paraprofessional license).
UT.climate_concern: Utah's combination of August 2020 Legal Innovation Sandbox launch (the second state to authorize non-attorney legal-services delivery, alongside AZ ABS reform), Licensed Paralegal Practitioner (LPP) credential under Rule 14-802, Salt Lake City + Provo metro concentration of 80%+ of licensed attorneys, integrated mandatory Utah State Bar membership, $59.25/hr BLS attorney mean (driven by Salt Lake City tech-corridor in-house and 'Silicon Slopes' growth), 4-year PI statute of limitations (above the 2-year median), and the Utah Bar Foundation IOLTA + Utah Legal Services funding.

VT.name: Vermont
VT.license_board_name: Vermont Board of Bar Examiners — Vermont Bar Association (voluntary)
VT.permit_typical: Vermont Supreme Court attorney admission mandatory; UBE 270 (median); 12 V.S.A. § 5901 UPL enforcement; mandatory CLE 20 credits every 2 years; IOLTA participation mandatory; Vermont Law Office Study Program (apprenticeship)
VT.distinctive_law: Vermont is one of only 4 U.S. states (alongside California, Virginia, and Washington) maintaining a Law Office Study Program (apprenticeship) pathway to bar admission — Vermont's program under Vermont Supreme Court Rule 7 allows applicants to qualify for the bar without attending law school by completing a 4-year apprenticeship under a Vermont attorney with at least 3 years of practice experience plus passing the Vermont Bar Exam (UBE), and Vermont Statutes 15 V.S.A. § 551 lists 6 grounds for divorce including 'living apart for 6 consecutive months and a resumption of marital relations is not reasonably probable' as a no-fault ground, plus Vermont adopted UBE with the median 270 minimum.
VT.climate_concern: Vermont's combination of Law Office Study Program (apprenticeship) bar admission alternative under Rule 7 (one of only 4 U.S. states maintaining this pathway), Burlington + Montpelier metro concentration of 60%+ of licensed attorneys leaving Northeast Kingdom rural counties with thin counsel coverage, voluntary Vermont Bar Association structure, $51.84/hr BLS attorney mean, and the Vermont Bar Foundation IOLTA + Vermont Legal Aid funding.

VA.name: Virginia
VA.license_board_name: Virginia State Bar (integrated mandatory bar) — Virginia Board of Bar Examiners administers Virginia Bar Exam (non-UBE)
VA.permit_typical: Virginia State Bar membership mandatory; Virginia Bar Exam (non-UBE — Virginia-specific essays on VA Civil Procedure, VA Family Law, VA Wills/Trusts/Estates, VA Real Property, VA Domestic Relations); Va. Code § 54.1-3904 aggressive UPL enforcement; mandatory annual MCLE 12 hours plus 2 ethics; IOLTA participation mandatory; Virginia Law Reader Program (apprenticeship)
VA.distinctive_law: Virginia is one of only 4 U.S. states (alongside California, Vermont, and Washington) maintaining a Law Reader Program (apprenticeship) pathway to bar admission — Virginia's program under Virginia Code § 54.1-3925 allows applicants to qualify for the Virginia Bar Exam without attending law school by completing a 3-year apprenticeship under a Virginia attorney with at least 10 years of practice — and Virginia is also one of the 12 non-UBE jurisdictions still operating a state-specific bar exam, plus Virginia Code § 20-91 requires 6 months separation (12 months if minor children) before no-fault divorce filing, plus Virginia Code § 20-97 requires 6 months Virginia residency before filing.
VA.climate_concern: Virginia's combination of Law Reader Program (apprenticeship) bar admission alternative under § 54.1-3925 (one of only 4 U.S. states maintaining this pathway), non-UBE Virginia Bar Exam, 6-12 month separation requirement before no-fault divorce filing, Northern Virginia (DC suburbs) + Richmond + Hampton Roads metro concentration of 80%+ of licensed attorneys, $73.50/hr BLS attorney mean (driven by NoVA federal-government-contractor in-house and Richmond banking bar), integrated mandatory Virginia State Bar, and the Virginia Law Foundation IOLTA + Virginia Legal Aid Society funding.

WA.name: Washington
WA.license_board_name: Washington State Bar Association (integrated mandatory bar) — Washington State Board of Bar Examiners administers UBE
WA.permit_typical: Washington State Bar Association membership mandatory; UBE 270 (median); RCW § 2.48.180 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual MCLE 45 credits every 3 years; IOLTA participation mandatory; Washington Law Clerk Program (apprenticeship); Limited License Legal Technician (LLLT) — phased out 2022
WA.distinctive_law: Washington pioneered the Limited License Legal Technician (LLLT) — the first non-attorney legal-services license in the U.S. (launched 2015 for family-law practice) — though the program was phased out in 2022 after the Washington State Supreme Court determined the demand and pipeline did not justify continuation; Washington remains one of only 4 U.S. states with a Law Clerk Program (apprenticeship) pathway to bar admission under APR 6 (alongside CA, VT, VA), plus Washington is one of only two states (alongside South Dakota) with NO statutory minimum residency period for divorce filing under RCW § 26.09.030 — petitioner need only be 'a resident of this state' at the time of filing.
WA.climate_concern: Washington's combination of pioneering Limited License Legal Technician (LLLT) program (the first non-attorney legal-services license in the U.S., 2015-2022 lifecycle), Law Clerk Program (apprenticeship) bar admission alternative under APR 6 (one of only 4 U.S. states maintaining this pathway), no statutory minimum residency period for divorce filing (one of only 2 states alongside SD), Seattle + Tacoma + Spokane metro concentration of 80%+ of licensed attorneys, $78.94/hr BLS attorney mean (driven by Seattle Big Tech in-house and Microsoft + Amazon corporate bar), integrated mandatory WSBA membership, and the Washington Legal Foundation IOLTA + Equal Justice Coalition robust funding tier.

WV.name: West Virginia
WV.license_board_name: West Virginia State Bar (integrated mandatory bar) — West Virginia Board of Law Examiners administers UBE
WV.permit_typical: West Virginia State Bar membership mandatory; UBE 270 (median); WV Code § 30-2-4 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual MCLE 24 credits every 2 years; IOLTA participation mandatory
WV.distinctive_law: West Virginia has one of the lowest BLS attorney mean hourly wages in the country at $44.50/hr — the lowest in Appalachia and among the lowest 5 states nationwide — and West Virginia Code § 48-5-202 lists 'irreconcilable differences' as the no-fault ground alongside 7 fault grounds (adultery, conviction of a felony, cruel or inhuman treatment, drunkenness, willful abandonment for 6 months, abuse, and incurable insanity), plus West Virginia requires 1 year of West Virginia residency before filing under WV Code § 48-5-105 and a 1-year separation period if proceeding under the 'living separate and apart' alternative no-fault ground.
WV.climate_concern: West Virginia's combination of $44.50/hr BLS attorney mean (the lowest in Appalachia and among the lowest 5 states in the country), 1-year residency requirement before divorce filing plus 1-year separation alternative no-fault ground, Charleston + Morgantown + Wheeling metro concentration of 60%+ of licensed attorneys leaving the southern coal counties with thin counsel coverage, integrated mandatory West Virginia State Bar membership, and the WV Bar Foundation IOLTA + WV Legal Aid Foundation modest funding base relative to coal-county economic-distress legal-need volume.

WI.name: Wisconsin
WI.license_board_name: State Bar of Wisconsin (integrated mandatory bar) — Wisconsin Board of Bar Examiners administers UBE for non-Wisconsin-law-school graduates; diploma privilege for UW-Madison and Marquette grads
WI.permit_typical: State Bar of Wisconsin membership mandatory; DIPLOMA PRIVILEGE for graduates of University of Wisconsin Law School and Marquette University Law School (admitted without bar exam); UBE 260 for graduates of all other law schools; Wis. Stat. § 757.30 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual MCLE 30 credits every 2 years; IOLTA participation mandatory
WI.distinctive_law: Wisconsin is the ONLY U.S. state operating a Diploma Privilege bar admission pathway — graduates of the University of Wisconsin Law School (UW-Madison) and Marquette University Law School are admitted to the State Bar of Wisconsin WITHOUT sitting for any bar exam under Wisconsin Supreme Court Rule SCR 40.03 (Diploma Privilege has been in continuous operation in Wisconsin since 1870 — the longest-running bar admission alternative in the country) — and Wis. Stat. § 767.07 lists 'irretrievable breakdown of the marriage' as the sole no-fault ground for divorce, plus Wisconsin operates a community-property regime under Wis. Stat. § 766.31 (one of only 9 community-property states in the U.S. and the only one in the Midwest).
WI.climate_concern: Wisconsin's combination of UNIQUE Diploma Privilege admission for UW-Madison and Marquette law-school graduates (the only diploma-privilege jurisdiction in the U.S., in continuous operation since 1870), community-property regime (the only community-property state in the Midwest, one of 9 nationwide), Milwaukee + Madison metro concentration of 65%+ of licensed attorneys, integrated mandatory State Bar of Wisconsin membership, $52.84/hr BLS attorney mean, and the Wisconsin IOLTA Foundation + Wisconsin Trust Account Foundation funding base.

WY.name: Wyoming
WY.license_board_name: Wyoming State Bar (integrated mandatory bar) — Wyoming State Board of Law Examiners administers UBE
WY.permit_typical: Wyoming State Bar membership mandatory; UBE 270 (median); WS § 33-5-117 UPL enforcement; mandatory annual MCLE 15 credits; IOLTA participation mandatory
WY.distinctive_law: Wyoming has the lowest divorce filing fees in the country at $85 (court filing fee under W.S. § 5-3-206) — plus modest summons-and-service costs — making Wyoming uncontested divorce one of the most affordable in the country at $400-$2,200 total typical attorney + filing combined, and W.S. § 20-2-104 lists 'irreconcilable differences' as the sole no-fault ground for divorce with 60 days of Wyoming residency required, plus Wyoming State Bar maintains an active Rural Practice Initiative providing technology grants and CLE programming for solo practitioners in counties with fewer than 10 attorneys (covering most of Wyoming's 23 counties).
WY.climate_concern: Wyoming's combination of lowest divorce filing fees in the country at $85 (driving the lowest typical uncontested divorce range in the U.S. at $400-$2,200 total), Cheyenne + Casper metro concentration of 50%+ of licensed attorneys leaving most of Wyoming's 23 counties with sub-1-attorney-per-1000-residents access ratios, Wyoming State Bar Rural Practice Initiative providing tech grants and CLE for solo rural practitioners, integrated mandatory Wyoming State Bar membership, $52.45/hr BLS attorney mean, and the Wyoming Equal Justice Wyoming Foundation IOLTA + Wyoming State Bar Pro Bono Program.

DC.name: District of Columbia
DC.license_board_name: District of Columbia Bar (integrated mandatory bar) — DC Court of Appeals Committee on Admissions administers UBE
DC.permit_typical: District of Columbia Bar membership mandatory; UBE 266; DC Code § 22-2603 UPL enforcement; mandatory CLE: NO mandatory CLE requirement (DC is one of 4 jurisdictions); IOLTA participation mandatory; mandatory pre-admission Course on the District of Columbia Practice
DC.distinctive_law: The District of Columbia has the highest BLS attorney mean hourly wage in the country at $97.32/hr — driven by the federal-government-litigation, lobbying, regulatory-counsel, and Big Law concentration in DC — and DC Bar requires every newly admitted attorney to complete the mandatory Course on the District of Columbia Practice (CDCP) covering DC-specific Civil Procedure, DC Family Law, DC Criminal Law, and DC Professional Responsibility before admission, plus DC is one of only 4 U.S. jurisdictions (alongside MD, MA, MI, SD) with NO mandatory continuing legal education (CLE) requirement, and DC Code § 16-904 lists 'mutual and voluntary separation for 6 months' as the sole no-fault ground.
DC.climate_concern: District of Columbia's combination of $97.32/hr BLS attorney mean (the highest in the country, driven by federal-government and Big Law concentration), NO mandatory CLE requirement (one of only 4 U.S. jurisdictions), mandatory pre-admission Course on the District of Columbia Practice (CDCP), 6-month mutual separation requirement before no-fault divorce filing under § 16-904, integrated mandatory DC Bar membership (the largest DC-area bar at 100,000+ active members), and the DC Bar Foundation IOLTA + DC Access to Justice Commission robust funding tier.
